description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a slightly bulging cylindrical shape, constructed from vertical wooden staves bound together by six evenly spaced, dark metallic hoops. The wood appears weathered and textured, with visible grain patterns and some discoloration, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The metal bands are tarnished and mottled, likely iron or steel, showing signs of rust and wear. The top and bottom surfaces are flat and made of the same wood as the sides, with no visible lid or opening mechanism. The interior is not visible, but the construction implies it is hollow and capable of holding liquids or solids. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban environments for storage, decoration, or waste collection.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with six dark metal hoops, bulging sides, and a flat wooden top and bottom. The wood is textured and aged, with no visible lid or handles.
description_view_0: The barrel's front view shows the curvature of the staves and the texture of the wood, with all six metal hoops clearly visible.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the barrel's bulging midsection and the weathered appearance of the metal bands.
description_view_2: The opposite side view displays similar features, with the wood grain and metal hoops prominent and no handles or markings visible.
description_view_3: This rear view shows the same construction details, with the metal hoops and wood texture consistent around the barrel.
